title: Landscaping Project Additional Information - Markland POA
description: Please provide detailed information about your landscaping project to ensure compliance with Markland Design Standards and Guidelines and community environmental sustainability requirements

primary.summary: Comprehensive landscape requirements covering all aspects of residential landscaping including plant selection, irrigation, foundation plantings, tree requirements, and maintenance standards. All landscaping work requires ARB approval through the Design Review Application process.
keyRequirements: All landscape modifications require ARB application and approval before installation
Plant selection must focus on low water demand species from the recommended plant palette
Foundation planting must include both 3-gallon and 7-gallon shrubs with specific height and spacing requirements
All areas from roadway curb to property lines must receive landscape treatment
Specific minimum tree caliper inch requirements per lot type must be met
St. Augustine or Zoysia grass required for all sod zones
Irrigation must provide 100% coverage with automatic controllers and rain sensors recommended
primary.quote: Plant selection shall focus on low water demand species as indicated in the plant palette and landscape zones found in these guidelines.

additionalReferences.summary: Detailed submittal requirements for landscape plans including scale, plant specifications, and compliance documentation
keyProvisions: Plans must show accurate lot dimensions with 1"=10' or 1"=20' scale
Location and common names of all new landscape material required
Table accounting for St. Johns County tree credit requirements

additionalReferences.summary: Extensive approved plant list organized by categories including large trees, small/medium trees, palms, shrubs, groundcovers, and native grasses
keyProvisions: Alternative species acceptable subject to ARB review
Native and ornamental grasses section included
Turf limited to St. Augustine (residential) and Zoysia (common areas)

fields.label: Markland Lot Type Classification
options: Traditional Type 1 Lots (58 ft. width - rear garage access)
Traditional Type 2 Lots (73 ft. width - rear garages)
fields.description: Your lot type determines specific landscape requirements and restrictions

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 3 - Lot Types and Section 8 - Special Lot Conditions
relevantBylaws.requirement: Different lot types have specific landscape requirements, setbacks, and plant placement rules
relevantBylaws.note: Lakefront lots require 4 trees minimum in rear yard within 20 feet of lake easement. Back to Back lots require rear buffer plantings within 15 feet of rear property line.
relevantBylaws.quote: There are a variety of housing types, lot sizes, lot orientations and special lot conditions in Markland. The relevance of this diversity in the context of these standards is their application throughout the project.

fields.label: ARB Application Status
options: Not yet submitted - planning to submit
fields.description: All landscaping modifications require Architectural Review Board approval before installation

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 2.1 - New Construction and Section 2.4 - Landscape Plan Requirements
relevantBylaws.requirement: No construction or landscaping work can commence until applicants receive final ARB approval
relevantBylaws.note: Landscape plans must include location, caliper, size and common names of all new landscape material
relevantBylaws.quote: No construction of any kind shall commence until applicants receive their final approval.

fields.label: Primary Type of Landscaping Work
fields.description: Select the primary type of landscaping work being performed

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.1 - General Landscape Design Requirements and Section 5.8 - Vegetable Gardens
relevantBylaws.requirement: All landscape work must comply with specific ARB standards and use approved plant materials
relevantBylaws.note: Vegetable gardens must be integrated into landscape composition with screening from lakefront views
relevantBylaws.quote: Plant selection shall focus on low water demand species as indicated in the plant palette and landscape zones found in these guidelines.

fields.label: Additional Work Included
fields.description: Check any additional landscaping work being performed

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.9 - Mulch/Ornamental Landscape Edging
relevantBylaws.requirement: All mulch and edging materials must be approved by ARB according to community standards
relevantBylaws.note: Landscape edging reveal must be no higher than 1 inch above mulch or lawn surface

fields.label: Areas of Property Being Landscaped
fields.description: Select all areas of the property that will be landscaped

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.1(l) - All areas of the lot from the roadway curb to all the side and rear property lines shall receive landscape treatment
relevantBylaws.requirement: Complete landscape treatment required from street to all property boundaries
relevantBylaws.note: Foundation shrubs must wrap around all four corners and be planted along entirety of all four walls

fields.label: Total Area Size
fields.description: Approximate total area being landscaped in square feet or acres

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.1(d) - Minimum 30% of roadway frontage must be landscape bed
relevantBylaws.requirement: Landscape beds with ground cover plants required along roadway edge for conventional lots
relevantBylaws.note: Removal of required landscape beds subject to ARB approval according to community standards

fields.label: Landscape Design Style
fields.description: What style best describes your landscaping design approach?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.10 - Recommended Plant Palette
relevantBylaws.requirement: Alternative species are acceptable and subject to review by the ARB
relevantBylaws.note: Plant palette focuses on low water demand species suitable for local climate conditions

fields.label: Tree Specifications (if applicable)
fields.placeholder:
Species: Live Oak (Quercus virginiana)
Quantity: 2
Size at planting: 4.5 inch caliper, 14-16 ft height
Mature size: 40-60 feet tall, 60-80 feet wide
Location: Front yard corners, 15 feet from house
fields.description: List each tree species, quantity, planting size, mature size, and specific locations

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.4 - Street Tree Plantings and Section 5.5 - Minimum Tree Requirements
relevantBylaws.requirement: Live oaks: 14-16 ft height, minimum 4.5 inch caliper, Florida Fancy Grade A required for street trees
relevantBylaws.note: Each lot has specific minimum tree caliper inch requirements that must be met per St. Johns County requirements
relevantBylaws.quote: The number of street trees for each lot varies based on location and lot width.

fields.label: Shrub and Bush Specifications
fields.placeholder:
Species: Firebush (Hamelia patens)
Quantity: 10
Size at planting: 3-gallon containers
Mature size: 6-8 feet tall, 4-6 feet wide
Spacing: 4 feet on center
fields.description: List shrub species, quantities, sizes, and spacing details

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.1(c) - Foundation Planting Requirements
relevantBylaws.requirement: Foundation planting must include both 3-gallon and 7-gallon shrubs with 36" minimum height within 20 feet of front corner
relevantBylaws.note: 3-gallon plants installed 18" on center max, 7-gallon plants 36" on center max, staggered grid pattern required
relevantBylaws.quote: Foundation planting must include both three (3) gallon and seven (7) gallon shrubs.

fields.label: Perennial and Flower Specifications
fields.placeholder:
Species: Coontie (Zamia floridana)
Quantity: 15
Size at planting: 1-gallon containers
Mature size: 3 feet tall, 4 feet wide
Bloom period: N/A (cycad)
fields.description: List perennial flowers, ornamental grasses, and other plantings

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.10 - Recommended Plant Palette includes extensive groundcover and native grass options
relevantBylaws.requirement: Plants must be selected from approved palette or submitted for ARB review
relevantBylaws.note: Native grasses section includes Muhly Grass, Fakahatchee Grass, and other approved species

fields.label: Grass/Lawn Specifications
fields.placeholder:
Type: St. Augustine grass (per Markland requirements)
Area: 1,200 square feet
Installation method: Sod
Irrigation requirements: 100% coverage automatic system
fields.description: Specify grass type, area coverage, and installation method

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.1(b) - Turf Requirements
relevantBylaws.requirement: St. Augustine or Zoysia grass shall be used for all sod zones on home lots for full and immediate coverage
relevantBylaws.note: Zoysia sod used in common areas and neighborhood parks. Encourage minimizing turf area to reduce water demand
relevantBylaws.quote: St. Augustine or Zoysia grass shall be used for all sod zones on home lots and lake bank edges where required for full and immediate coverage at Markland.

fields.label: Hardscape Materials (if applicable)
fields.placeholder:
Material: Natural flagstone
Area: 200 square feet walkway
Base: 4-inch compacted gravel base
Joints: Polymeric sand
fields.description: Describe any pavers, stones, concrete, or other hardscape materials

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 7.5 - Sidewalks and Other Pedestrian Paving
relevantBylaws.requirement: All horizontal site paving that encroaches into property setbacks requires ARB approval
relevantBylaws.note: Arrival areas and prominent walkways encouraged to use brick, stone, colored concrete, or patterned materials

fields.label: Mature Size and Spacing Plan
fields.placeholder: Explain how plant placement accounts for mature sizes, utility clearances, and sight lines per Markland requirements...
fields.description: Demonstrate understanding of how plants will grow and interact at maturity

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.1(f)(g)(h) - Foundation and Layout Requirements
relevantBylaws.requirement: Foundation beds must undulate in shape with sufficient depth, wrap around all building corners, and account for mature plant sizes
relevantBylaws.note: Two-tiered landscaping required where foundation walls exceed 3 feet in height

fields.label: Percentage of Native Plants
fields.description: Native plants support local wildlife and require less water and maintenance

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.10 - Recommended Plant Palette includes extensive native options
relevantBylaws.requirement: Recommended palette emphasizes native Florida species adapted to local conditions
relevantBylaws.note: Native options include Live Oak, Bald Cypress, Coontie, and native grasses like Muhly Grass

fields.label: Water Management Strategy
options: Mixed water needs with zoned irrigation
fields.description: How will the landscape manage water usage and conservation?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.1(a) and Section 5.6 - Water Conservation Requirements
relevantBylaws.requirement: Plant selection shall focus on low water demand species. Irrigation systems must be designed for water efficiency
relevantBylaws.note: Soil moisture sensing and rain sensing override devices are recommended for automatic controllers
relevantBylaws.quote: Every effort should be made to implement water conservation techniques in both the landscape design and the design and operation of the irrigation system.

fields.label: Existing Soil Conditions
fields.placeholder: Sandy soil, well-draining, pH 6.5, needs organic matter amendment per local conditions...
fields.description: Describe current soil type, drainage, pH, and any amendments needed

fields.label: Drainage System Modifications
fields.placeholder: Installation of French drain along property line, regrading for positive drainage per Markland drainage requirements...
fields.description: Describe any changes to drainage patterns, grading, or stormwater management

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.6 - Earthwork and Drainage
relevantBylaws.requirement: Positive drainage must be provided and cannot be diverted onto adjacent lots. Drainage patterns must comply with Neighborhood Grading and Drainage Plan
relevantBylaws.note: Landscape mounding limited to 1.5 feet height with smooth transitions and cannot disturb swale drainage
relevantBylaws.quote: Positive drainage must be provided and drainage cannot be diverted or directed onto adjacent lots.

fields.label: Existing Tree Preservation
fields.placeholder: Protecting existing 30-inch oak tree with 10-foot protection zone per Markland tree protection standards...
fields.description: Plans for protecting or working around existing significant trees

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.5 - Site Preparation - Existing Vegetation and Tree Protection
relevantBylaws.requirement: Protected trees must be identified, located, and properly protected with silt barriers. Tree protection fencing required per St. Johns County regulations
relevantBylaws.note: Filling, grading and disturbances not permitted within drip line of protected tree species
relevantBylaws.quote: Prior to site work those protected trees to remain must be identified on plans, located on site and properly protected with silt barriers.

fields.label: Wildlife and Pollinator Benefits
options: Native plant species for local wildlife
fields.description: Ways the landscape will support local wildlife and pollinators

fields.label: Installation Method
fields.description: Who will be performing the landscaping installation?

fields.label: Landscaping Contractor Information
fields.description: Name and license number of landscaping contractor (if using professional)

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.1 - Responsibility for Compliance
relevantBylaws.requirement: Fee simple property owner ultimately responsible for compliance with all guidelines and applicable codes
relevantBylaws.note: All contractors must comply with construction standards and community requirements

fields.label: Contractor Insurance Information
fields.description: Contractor liability insurance details (required for professional installation)

fields.label: Installation Start Date
fields.description: When will landscaping installation begin?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 2.1 - No construction of any kind shall commence until applicants receive their final approval
relevantBylaws.requirement: ARB approval must be received before any work begins
relevantBylaws.note: Site work cannot commence until site plan approved and builder has met with Developer representative onsite

fields.label: Expected Completion Date
fields.description: When will all landscaping work be completed?

fields.label: Seasonal Timing Considerations
fields.placeholder: Planting trees in fall for better root establishment, installing cool-season annuals per Florida climate...
fields.description: How does the timing align with optimal planting seasons for different plants?

fields.label: Installation Phases
fields.placeholder: Phase 1: Site prep and hardscape (Week 1), Phase 2: Tree and shrub planting (Week 2), Phase 3: Flowers and mulch (Week 3)
fields.description: Outline installation phases and timeline if work will be done in stages

fields.label: Irrigation System
fields.description: How will the landscaped areas be watered?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.6 - Irrigation Requirements
relevantBylaws.requirement: Irrigation shall provide 100% coverage of all turf zones and all new planted areas. Automatic irrigation controllers required with separate programs for different irrigation needs
relevantBylaws.note: System must deliver water efficiently and uniformly with proper sprinkler head placement to minimize overspray
relevantBylaws.quote: Irrigation shall provide 100% coverage of all turf zones; all new planted areas including turf, tree, shrub and groundcover plantings shall be irrigated.

fields.label: Irrigation Design Details
fields.placeholder: 3 new zones added to existing controller, drip lines for shrub beds, pop-up sprinklers for lawn areas per Markland standards...
fields.description: Describe irrigation system design, zones, and coverage (if applicable)

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.6 - Irrigation design must deliver water based on specific water demand of each planting zone
relevantBylaws.requirement: System designed with zones for different irrigation needs. Drip emitters, soakers and bubblers recommended for trees
relevantBylaws.note: Soil moisture sensing and rain sensing override devices are recommended for water conservation

fields.label: Water Source
fields.description: What water source will be used for irrigation?

fields.label: Long-term Maintenance Schedule
fields.placeholder: Monthly: pruning and deadheading, Quarterly: fertilization, Annually: mulch refresh, tree pruning per Markland standards...
fields.description: Detailed plan for ongoing landscape maintenance including timing and responsibilities

fields.label: Maintenance Responsibility
fields.description: Who will be responsible for ongoing landscape maintenance?

fields.label: ARB Plant List Compliance
options: All plants are on ARB approved list
Most plants are approved, some need approval
fields.description: How do your plant selections align with Markland Design Standards plant palette?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.10 - Recommended Plant Palette
relevantBylaws.requirement: Alternative species are acceptable and subject to review by the ARB
relevantBylaws.note: Extensive approved plant list includes native and adapted species organized by size and function
relevantBylaws.quote: The recommended plant palette is intended to provide the Owner/Builder a baseline for planting. Alternative species are acceptable and subject to review by the ARB.

fields.label: Utility Line Clearance
fields.description: Have underground utilities been located for any digging work?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.2.3(l) - Owner/Builder responsible for ensuring no disruption to existing utility systems
relevantBylaws.requirement: Must call for utility locates as required to avoid damage to existing utilities
relevantBylaws.note: Cost for repairs to utility damage paid by Owner/Builder that caused the damage

fields.label: Property Setback Compliance
fields.placeholder: All plantings maintain required setbacks per Markland lot type requirements, trees placed per street tree spacing guidelines...
fields.description: How do plantings comply with property line setbacks and easements?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.7 - Easements and Section 6.2.4 - Setbacks
relevantBylaws.requirement: ARB approval required for fences and plantings erected within easements. Must comply with minimum setbacks per approved engineering plans
relevantBylaws.note: Different restrictions apply to drainage easements, landscape easements, FPL easements, and lake maintenance easements

fields.label: Neighbor Impact Assessment
fields.placeholder: New trees will not block neighbors' views, drainage improvements will not affect adjacent properties per Markland requirements...
fields.description: Assess potential impacts on neighboring properties including views, drainage, and root systems

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 4.6(b) - Positive drainage must be provided and drainage cannot be diverted onto adjacent lots
relevantBylaws.requirement: Cannot redirect drainage to neighboring properties. Grading not permitted outside property line
relevantBylaws.note: Adjustments to side yard conditions may be necessary to ensure positive drainage as adjacent lots are developed

fields.label: Special Lot Condition Requirements
options: Lakefront lot - 4 tree minimum in rear yard
Back to back lot - rear buffer planting required
Conservation lot - silt fence protection required
Corner lot - side elevation architectural treatment needed
Perimeter lot - masonry wall maintenance clearance
Traditional Type 1/2 lot - specific landscape template required
fields.description: Check any special lot conditions that apply to your property

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 8 - Special Lot Conditions
relevantBylaws.requirement: Various special conditions apply specific landscape requirements based on lot location and type
relevantBylaws.note: Each special condition has detailed requirements for plantings, setbacks, and design treatments

fields.label: Permits Required
options: ARB Design Review Application (required for all work)
fields.description: Check any permits required for this landscaping project

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 2.2 - Application Form & Fees
relevantBylaws.requirement: Completed Design Review Application Form required. Refundable Performance Deposit required
relevantBylaws.note: Performance Deposit refundable at successful completion and compliance with Design Standards and Guidelines

fields.label: 5-Year Landscape Vision
fields.placeholder: Mature tree canopy providing shade, established shrub beds requiring minimal maintenance, naturalized areas per Markland character...
fields.description: Describe how you envision the landscape will look and function in 5 years

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.1(a) - Focus on low water demand species and water conservation
relevantBylaws.requirement: Long-term planning should emphasize sustainable, low-maintenance landscapes that support community character
relevantBylaws.note: Consider mature plant sizes and ongoing maintenance requirements in planning

fields.label: Sustainability Features
options: Reduced lawn area for lower maintenance
fields.description: Sustainable landscape features being incorporated

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 5.6 - Water conservation techniques encouraged in landscape design and irrigation operation
relevantBylaws.requirement: Implementation of water conservation techniques encouraged
relevantBylaws.note: Soil moisture sensing and rain sensing override devices recommended for irrigation systems

fields.label: Project Budget Range
fields.description: Approximate budget range for the landscaping project (optional)

fields.label: Performance Deposit Understanding
options: Understand deposit required and refund process
Need more information about deposit requirements
Unsure about deposit amount for this project
fields.description: Do you understand the ARB Performance Deposit requirements?

relevantBylaws.reference: Section 2.2 - Refundable Performance Deposit
relevantBylaws.requirement: Refundable Performance Deposit required, amount posted on Design Review Application Form
relevantBylaws.note: Deposit refundable at successful completion of all work and compliance with Design Standards and Guidelines
relevantBylaws.quote: The Performance Deposit is refundable at successful completion of all work and compliance with the Design Standards and Guidelines.

required_documents: Completed Markland ARB Design Review Application Form
Landscape plan with accurate lot dimensions, north arrow and scale (1"=10' or 1"=20')
Complete plant list with botanical names, quantities, sizes, and caliper specifications
Location and common names of all existing trees to be saved
Table accounting for compliance with St. Johns County tree credit requirements
Property lines, easement lines, setback lines clearly marked
Irrigation system design with zone layout (if applicable)
Professional landscaper license and insurance (if using contractor)
ARB Performance Deposit (amount per application form)
Digital samples of mulch and hardscape materials for ARB approval

criticalReminders: NO WORK CAN BEGIN WITHOUT ARB APPROVAL - All landscaping modifications require completed Design Review Application and ARB approval before installation
Performance Deposit Required - Refundable deposit required per application form, refunded upon successful completion and compliance inspection
Plant Palette Compliance - All plants must be from approved Markland plant palette or submitted for ARB review and approval
St. Augustine or Zoysia Grass Only - Required for all sod zones on home lots for immediate, full coverage
100% Irrigation Coverage Required - All new planted areas including turf, trees, shrubs and groundcover must be irrigated
Foundation Planting Standards - Must include both 3-gallon and 7-gallon shrubs with specific height and spacing requirements
Lot-Specific Tree Requirements - Each lot has minimum tree caliper inch requirements that must be met per St. Johns County standards
Special Lot Conditions - Lakefront, corner, conservation and other special lots have additional specific requirements
Drainage Compliance - Cannot redirect drainage to neighboring properties, must maintain positive drainage patterns
Utility Clearance Required - Must call 811 and locate utilities before any digging work begins
commonViolations: Starting work before ARB approval received
Using non-approved plant species without ARB review
Installing wrong grass type (must be St. Augustine or Zoysia)
Inadequate irrigation coverage for new plantings
Incorrect foundation planting sizing and spacing
Not meeting minimum tree caliper inch requirements
Improper setbacks from property lines and easements
Not protecting existing trees during construction
Using non-approved mulch or hardscape materials
Failing to complete work within approved timeline
Not scheduling compliance inspection before deposit refund
